section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports tenderness on the medial joint line of his knee and reports no tenderness to palpation of the pes bursa or popliteal space.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Detailed localization of tenderness and absence of tenderness to palpation are typically derived from clinician physical exam, not purely patient report; placing them in S blurs exam vs history and could mislead about objective findings.

section_miscategorizations.text: He also reports a dime-size abrasion over the medial patella and reports abrasions on the back of his knee.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Skin abrasions are observable exam findings; while the patient can describe them, they are more appropriately documented in the objective exam section.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports a dime-size abrasion on the medial patella.
section_miscategorizations.reason: This sentence is explicitly framed as a patient report rather than an observed finding; in the objective section it should be rephrased as an observed abrasion or moved to S.

section_miscategorizations.text: The patient reports mild laxity and a slight posterior drawer test.
section_miscategorizations.reason: Laxity and posterior drawer test results are clinician-performed exam maneuvers; attributing them as patient-reported within the objective section is internally inconsistent and could confuse whether these are true exam findings.
Denials phrased as patient statements belong in the subjective review of systems; in O this should be documented as absence of crepitus on exam rather than a patient denial.

section_miscategorizations.text: The doctor will examine the patient's knee.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The knee exam is already described in the objective section; stating that the doctor will examine the knee as a future action in the plan is inappropriate and confusing, as exams performed belong in O and are not typically framed as future orders once completed.
